reasoning: Under 3A001.a.5 ADCs are controlled by resolution/sample-rate thresholds; for resolution greater than 14 bits the control trigger is an output rate exceeding ~0.4 GSPS (hundreds of MSPS). This 24-bit converter samples at only 256 kSPS (0.256 MSPS), far below any 3A001.a.5 threshold, so it is not described by that or any other CCL entry and is classified EAR99.

item_name: Quad RF-DAC / quad RF-ADC mixed-signal front-end
reasoning: The device integrates ADC channels at 4 GSPS (4000 MSPS), far exceeding the 3A001.a.5 thresholds for analog-to-digital converters (e.g., >1.0 GSPS at 12-bit resolution). It also contains 12 GSPS DACs that independently meet 3A001.a.6 DAC limits, but the high-speed ADC function squarely places this mixed-signal front-end under Category 3 microelectronics control 3A001.a.5.

item_name: 6 GSPS RF digital-to-analog converter, to 5 GHz
reasoning: The item is a digital-to-analog converter with 16-bit resolution and a 6 GSPS (6000 MSPS) update rate. ECCN 3A001.a.5.b controls DACs with a resolution of 14 bit or more and an adjusted update rate greater than 2,500 MSPS; this device exceeds both thresholds.

item_name: Wideband 2x2 RF agile transceiver, 47 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: Frequency-agile 2x2 transceiver covering 47 MHz-6 GHz with up to 56 MHz channel bandwidth and integrated DSP/data-converter baseband for software-defined radio falls under Cat 5 Part 1 telecom equipment; 5A001.b.3 controls radio equipment employing digital techniques (DSP) to provide frequency hopping/agile spread-spectrum operation. Subparagraph chosen over a Cat 3 component classification because the item is a complete tunable agile RF transceiver for SDR/wireless infrastructure rather than a standalone IC component.

A 300 MHz single-core 32-bit audio DSP for consumer/commercial audio processing (mixing, filtering, equalization) does not meet the performance thresholds or special design criteria of Category 3 microprocessor/microcircuit controls (3A001.a.3 et seq.). Its modest clock speed and audio-only application place it well below any ADP/microprocessor performance parameters, and it is not specially designed for controlled signal-processing applications. It is subject to the EAR but not described on the CCL, hence EAR99.

item_name: Narrow-band sub-1 GHz ISM RF transceiver
reasoning: This is a radio communications transceiver operating in sub-1 GHz ISM bands. As a standard low-power, narrow-band FSK/GFSK ISM-band RF transceiver for metering and wireless data links, it does not employ frequency hopping/spread spectrum beyond civil norms, but commercial wireless transceivers fall under Category 5 Part 1 telecommunications. However, given it is a mass-market low-power ISM device lacking controlled performance parameters (no specified encryption, no high power, standard civil modulation), it most likely classifies as EAR99 unless it incorporates controlled functionality. Absent additional controlled features, the device is best classified as EAR99.

item_name: Integrated dual-channel RF transceiver, 75 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: The item is a highly integrated RF transceiver IC intended for software-defined radio and wireless communications infrastructure (transmit/receive channels, on-chip synthesizers, digital filtering). As a telecommunications transceiver component operating 75 MHz–6 GHz, it falls in CCL Category 5 Part 1. It does not meet the specific control thresholds of 5A001 (e.g., dedicated spread-spectrum/anti-jam, channel-hop, or specialized SIGINT features), so it is captured under the residual telecommunications entry 5A991 for equipment/components not controlled by 5A001.

This is a general-purpose microprocessor/DSP system-on-chip (ARM Cortex-A5 plus two floating-point SHARC DSP cores) for commercial audio, industrial and signal-processing use. It is not a microwave/specialized military component, so it falls under 3A991.a.1 covering microprocessor microcircuits and related processing devices not controlled by 3A001. Such a commercial heterogeneous DSP/SoC does not meet the high-performance ADC/computing thresholds of 3A001.a.7, so 3A991 (anti-terrorism controlled) is the appropriate catch-all electronics classification.

The item is a hardware IC implementing cryptography for data confidentiality using AES symmetric and ECDH/ECDSA public-key algorithms exceeding the 56-bit symmetric / 512-bit asymmetric thresholds, classified under Category 5 Part 2 'Information Security.' As a commodity 'designed or modified to use cryptography employing digital techniques performing cryptographic functions other than authentication or digital signature,' it falls under 5A002.a (information security functions including secure key storage, TRNG, and secure-boot acceleration).

item_name: 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi + Bluetooth module, 4 MB flash
reasoning: This is a wireless communications module (Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n + Bluetooth/BLE) employing radio-frequency transceiver functionality. Such commercial short-range RF transceivers fall under Category 5 Part 1. If it incorporates encryption for the wireless link (typical for Wi-Fi/BLE), it is classified as a mass-market encryption item under 5A992.c; absent controlled-strength cryptography it would otherwise be EAR99, but standard Wi-Fi/Bluetooth security places it in 5A992.c.

FPGAs are integrated circuits controlled under Category 3 (Electronics). Programmable logic devices/field programmable logic devices fall under 3A001.a.7 as general-purpose ICs. This low-power, low-density FPGA does not meet advanced thresholds but is still described by the programmable logic device entry; absent meeting performance limits it remains classified under 3A001.a.7.

The secure element implements 'information security' cryptography for confidentiality using symmetric (AES-128) and asymmetric (ECDSA/ECDH elliptic curve) algorithms exceeding the technical thresholds in Note to 5A002.a (symmetric >56 bits, asymmetric ECC >112 bits). As a hardware component designed/modified to use cryptography employing digital techniques performing cryptographic functions, it is classified under 5A002.a (Category 5, Part 2).

A general-purpose 16-bit DSC running at 60 MIPS with integrated flash/RAM, USB, CAN, and DMA is a commercial microcontroller/DSP for motor control and embedded use. It does not meet the high-performance microprocessor/ADC thresholds or specialized parameters of Category 3 entries (e.g., 3A001.a.1-.7); ordinary commercial DSPs/microcontrollers are not specifically controlled and fall under EAR99.

This is a network communications switch (Layer-2 managed Ethernet switch) subject to the EAR under Category 5 Part 1 (telecommunications). It does not meet the higher performance/encryption thresholds of 5A001, so it falls under 5A991 as telecommunications equipment, specifically network switching/communications apparatus per 5A991.b.4. Basic 10/100 Layer-2 switching with no controlled cryptographic functionality places it in this general telecom-equipment classification rather than EAR99.

A standard 10/100 Ethernet PHY transceiver operating at 100 Mbps with an RMII interface is ordinary commercial telecommunications/networking infrastructure. It does not meet the thresholds or special functions controlled under Category 5 Part 1 (e.g., 5A001) such as exceeding the digital transfer rate limits or providing controlled cryptographic functionality, and it is not described by any other CCL entry, so it is EAR99.

A standard 10/100 Mbps Ethernet PHY transceiver provides commodity-grade physical-layer signaling well below any performance thresholds in Category 5 Part 1 (telecommunications) or Category 4. Such low-speed embedded networking ICs are not described by any specific CCL entry (e.g., 5A001 telecom equipment thresholds far exceed these speeds), so the item is subject to the EAR but classified EAR99.

The microcontroller integrates a hardware cryptographic engine implementing symmetric algorithms (AES, 3DES) with key lengths exceeding 56 bits, making it an item designed to use cryptography for data confidentiality. Under Category 5 Part 2, 5A002.a controls information security commodities employing such cryptographic functionality; the embedded crypto engine, not the MCU's general processing capability, drives the classification.

This is a short-range 2.4 GHz Bluetooth Low Energy wireless transceiver/radio module providing standard commercial connectivity. It is a telecommunications transmission/reception apparatus not meeting the higher-performance thresholds of 5A001. Such mass-market frequency-hopping/spread-spectrum radio equipment is classified under Category 5 Part 1 telecommunications, ECCN 5A991 (radio equipment), absent any controlled cryptographic functionality that would place it under 5A002.

item_name: Quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 compute module (2 GB / 16 GB)
reasoning: This is a general-purpose low-performance compute module (quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 at 1.5 GHz) that does not meet the high-performance APP thresholds of 4A003 nor any specialized digital computer controls. It falls under the basket category 4A994 for digital computers and assemblies not controlled elsewhere. The integrated wireless connectivity uses standard commercial Wi-Fi/Bluetooth and does not elevate the classification.

A general-purpose 32-bit ARM Cortex-M4 microcontroller running at 168 MHz with on-chip flash/SRAM is a commodity-grade embedded controller. It does not meet the performance thresholds or specialized criteria of Category 3 microprocessor/integrated-circuit entries (e.g., 3A001.a.3-.7), which target high-end APP, ADC/DAC, FPGA, RF, or radiation-hardened devices. Such standard MCUs are not described by any CCL entry and are therefore EAR99.

A standard 1-inch broadband dielectric mirror for the 400-750 nm visible band on fused-silica does not meet the specialized parameters of 6A004 (e.g., scanning/deformable mirrors, or mirrors with specific damage thresholds/flatness/coatings for laser systems). Ordinary visible turning mirrors are not enumerated on the CCL and are classified EAR99.

A two-adjuster kinematic mirror mount is a mechanical opto-mechanical alignment fixture providing manual tip/tilt adjustment. It contains no optics, lasers, or controlled materials and does not meet the technical thresholds of Category 6 optics (e.g., 6A004 mirrors/mounts for specialized deformable or beam-steering systems) or any other CCL entry. As a generic passive mechanical positioning component subject to the EAR but not described on the CCL, it is classified EAR99.

A single-core 32-bit ARM Cortex-A8 general-purpose applications microprocessor at 1.0 GHz does not meet the high-performance thresholds (e.g., 3A001.a.1 microprocessor metrics such as APP/SIMD/wordlength criteria) and is a commodity embedded processor not specified elsewhere on the CCL; thus it is subject to the EAR but classified EAR99.
